scope: Modern NIC QueueLease assurance subclaims for CapSched-H, using Intel ice as representative source evidence

evidence_classes.model_checked: Finite TLA+ evidence supporting local semantic constraints.
evidence_classes.source_observed: Linux source anchors showing where a future implementation might attach observation or enforcement.
evidence_classes.observation_only: Tracepoint/source readiness evidence; useful for study but not protection proof.
evidence_classes.readiness_gate: Evidence constraining a future implementation plan; useful for staging but not protection proof.
evidence_classes.protection_missing: Required monitor-backed or non-forgeable root is absent.
evidence_classes.forbidden: Claim or shortcut that must not be made from current evidence.

evidence_records.limitation: Every readiness row is observation_only=true, authority_claim=false, and monitor_verified=false.
Finite model checks revoke/drain/quarantine semantics; it is not real hardware or monitor enforcement evidence.
Source map for ice shutdown/rebuild anchors; not authority or protection evidence.
Observation-only formal/0031 obligation coverage for ice revoke paths; authority_claim=false and monitor_verified=false.
Finite model checks host/VF/monitor IRQ quiescence separation; not real interrupt-remapping or monitor enforcement evidence.
Finite model checks full route invalidation receipt semantics; not real hardware or monitor enforcement evidence.
Source map for ice, DMA API, IOMMU core, iommufd, VFIO, and arch-IOMMU invalidation substrate; not authority or protection evidence.
Finite model checks DMA/IOMMU/MemoryView invalidation receipt semantics; not real hardware or monitor enforcement evidence.
Source map for XSK CQ submission, XSK free-list return, page-pool recycle, and packet memory return substrate; not authority or protection evidence.
Finite model checks stale XSK/page-pool completion quarantine and packet memory return semantics; not real AF_XDP, page-pool, or monitor enforcement evidence.
Source map for ice representor, bridge/FDB/VLAN/switchdev, TC offload, BPF/TC redirect, and LAG lower_dev substrate; not authority or protection evidence.

Finite model checks representor-to-lower QueueLease derivation, bridge/FDB/VLAN non-authority, TC/offload control authority, stale lower_dev rebind, and representor-stop non-revoke semantics; not real bridge, TC, ice, or monitor enforcement evidence.

Source map for ice service task coalescing, reset/AdminQ/MailboxQ, VF virtchnl queue-control, PTP, DPLL, bridge/eswitch, LAG, GNSS, and reset/rebuild replay substrate; not authority or protection evidence.

Finite model checks service/caller authority intersection, per-effect carriers, coalesced service-loop merge hazards, PTP/DPLL control carriers, bridge/LAG/offload lower-lease requirements, and reset/rebuild replay freshness; not real driver or monitor enforcement evidence.

Source map for ice VF mailbox queue config, VF-provided descriptor-ring DMA address, queue enable, IRQ map, queue bandwidth/quanta, FDIR offload, and FDIR async completion substrate; not authority or protection evidence.

Finite model checks VF mailbox request, QueueControl, DMA ring-address, IRQ route, queue budget, FDIR offload, async completion, and revoke carrier separation; not real virtchnl, ice, IOMMU, MSI-X, or monitor enforcement evidence.

Source map for ice VF reset, teardown, MSI-X/vector remap, VSI generation, queue/DMA/IRQ config, FDIR async completion, mailbox dispatch, and service replay substrate; not authority or protection evidence.
Finite model checks VF id/VSI/queue/IRQ/DMA/FDIR/mailbox/service replay handoff freshness; not real SR-IOV, virtchnl, MSI-X, IOMMU, FDIR, or monitor enforcement evidence.

Architecture map for monitor roots, Linux service/driver Domain policy, typed target endpoints, receipt minting, local cluster lease compilation, and no per-packet monitor trap; not implementation or protection evidence.

Finite model checks monitor/service/target endpoint split, receipt minting, audit-only monitor hazards, remote lease local compilation, and no per-packet monitor trap; not real monitor, driver, IOMMU, IRQ, or performance evidence.
Maps each required receipt/carrier to observation-only probes or inert stubs; it is a readiness map, not implementation, authority, or protection evidence.

Finite model checks implementation-readiness gate ordering and rejects behavior approval, probe-as-authority, non-inert stubs, missing coverage, raw endpoint stubs, and protection claims; it is not real Linux compatibility or monitor enforcement evidence.
Defines the source-anchor ledger and no-code runner shape; it is observation planning, not authority or protection evidence.
Static runner emitted 37 rows with 36 available anchors, 1 expected LocalDomainDeviceLease external gap, and 0 safety flag violations; it is source observation only.
Defines the root-management/local monitor boundary for LocalDomainDeviceLease; it is not a Linux source anchor, monitor implementation, or protection claim.

Finite model checks local monitor compilation semantics and rejects ClusterLease direct use, scheduler placement authority, service admission minting, Linux device registration authority, stale epochs, wrong service/target Domains, queue receipt before local lease, and audit-only compile; it is not monitor, driver, IOMMU, IRQ, or protection evidence.

Defines the root-management/local monitor row contract, dependency sequence, safety flags, and forbidden authority collapses for LocalDomainDeviceLease observation evidence; it is not monitor implementation.
Validates the observation contract with 10 rows, 7 dependency rules, 0 dependency errors, 0 safety-flag violations, and 9 forbidden authority collapses; it is not protection evidence.

subclaims.title: Queue identity and binding
subclaims.claim: Queue effects require live QueueTag, queue epoch, ring, q_vector, NAPI instance, IRQ route, and queue budget.
protection_missing: VF epoch and VSI generation emission/check
forbidden: netdev/ring/q_vector/NAPI reachability as QueueLease authority
vf_id equality, ice_vf pointer reachability, or stable VSI index as QueueLease authority

subclaims.title: Typed submit classes
subclaims.claim: SKB, XDP frame, XDP_TX page-pool, and AF_XDP zero-copy submits are different authority classes.
forbidden: XDP_TX or AF_XDP zero-copy authorized by SKB authority
AF_XDP descriptor use by ambient driver state

subclaims.title: Descriptor publication and doorbell
subclaims.claim: Descriptor writes, next_to_watch publication, in-flight DMA, and tail doorbell consume a typed DescriptorLedger.
forbidden: ice tracepoint or Linux ring index as the ledger itself

subclaims.title: DMA packet memory ownership
subclaims.claim: DMA-capable packet memory requires MemoryView/IOMMU and class-specific memory authority.
protection_missing: real monitor-backed DMA invalidation receipt implementation
VF mailbox QueueConfigCarrier and DMA ring-address carrier implementation
VF epoch handoff DMA/IOMMU receipt implementation
HyperTag monitor-owned DMA receipt minting implementation
forbidden: Linux DMA mapping success as Domain authorization proof
IRQ invalidation as DMA revocation proof
dma_unmap_* or xsk_pool_dma_unmap return as DMA revocation proof
iommu_unmap_fast or queued IOVA flush as stale-translation invalidation proof
iommufd IOAS unmap or VFIO dma_unmap callback as PageOwner transfer proof
xsk_tx_completed, xsk_buff_free, or page_pool recycle as packet return proof
VF-provided dma_ring_addr plus queue/ring validation as DMA MemoryView authority
vf_id equality, stable VSI index, or VF reset completion as DMA MemoryView freshness

subclaims.title: Completion settlement
subclaims.claim: Completion settles a typed ledger under service authority and service budget.
protection_missing: service BudgetTicket or service budget rule
forbidden: completion charged to last submitter without explicit settlement rule
worker task identity as caller authority

subclaims.title: QueueControl
subclaims.claim: Devlink/rate/scheduler/VF/SF/representor lifecycle operations require QueueControl authority.
protection_missing: monitor receipt consumption before hardware mutation
VF mailbox queue budget/quanta carrier implementation
forbidden: QueueControl authorized by RunCap
QueueControl authorized by plain netdev reachability
QueueControl authorized by CAP_NET_ADMIN alone
TC/switchdev hardware rule install treated as plain RepresentorForward
virtchnl validation or opcode allowlist as QueueControl authority
QoS caps, queue id checks, or traffic class checks as queue budget authority

subclaims.title: RepresentorForward
subclaims.claim: Representor transmit requires RepresentorForward authority plus a live lower QueueLease and service budget.
forbidden: representor netdev reachability as lower queue submit authority
bridge FDB hit or VLAN allow decision as lower QueueLease authority
metadata_dst existence as lower QueueLease proof
LAG lower_dev rewrite preserving lower QueueLease without rebind

subclaims.title: ServiceWork and async provenance
subclaims.claim: Service and maintenance work is service/kernel authority and cannot create caller-attributed effects without a typed carrier.
protection_missing: frozen VF request carrier propagation into async FDIR completion
VF epoch handoff carrier for reset/reassignment reopen
service Domain proof that policy parsing does not mint monitor roots
forbidden: generic workqueue execution as a CapSched authority hook
kernel-internal work treated as caller authority
worker identity as caller or queue authority
ICE_SERVICE_SCHED pending bit as carrier authority
virtchnl opcode allowlist as QueueLease or QueueControl authority
PTP/DPLL callback reachability as caller control authority
bridge/FDB/switchdev/LAG event as OffloadCap or lower QueueLease authority
reset/rebuild replay after revoke without fresh reauthorization
FDIR ctx_irq or ctx_done as frozen request authority
vf_id equality, ICE_VF_STATE_ACTIVE, ICE_VF_STATE_DIS, or vf->cfg_lock as VF Domain authority

subclaims.title: Revoke semantics
subclaims.claim: Queue revoke invalidates submit, descriptor, DMA, completion, control, representor, and service work paths before reuse or delivery.
protection_missing: monitor-backed VF IRQ route invalidation implementation
monitor-backed IRQ route invalidation receipt implementation
stale XSK/page-pool quarantine and packet generation reset implementation
representor lower QueueLease invalidation and TC/offload rule removal receipt implementation
VF mailbox request carrier and async FDIR context implementation
VF identity epoch, VSI generation, mailbox embargo, and service replay handoff implementation
forbidden: queue revocation inferred from Linux netdev down/reset alone
queue reassignment after IRQ invalidation but before DMA receipt
PageOwner transfer or packet page return before DMA receipt
normal XSK CQ completion or page-pool recycle after revoke
representor netdev queue stop treated as lower QueueLease revoke
TC/switchdev stale hardware rule left live after lower QueueLease revoke
VF mailbox queue, IRQ, budget, or FDIR effect after revoke without fresh authorization
visible vf_id, stable VSI index, queue id, vector id, or old allowlist state as fresh handoff authority
service Domain policy, Linux DMA/IRQ state, signed cluster lease text, audit-only monitor logging, or raw PF/VF/IOMMU/MSI/devlink handles as monitor roots or receipts

subclaims.title: Linux substrate compatibility
subclaims.claim: Linux netdev, NAPI, XDP, AF_XDP, devlink, representor, workqueue, and tracepoints are compatibility substrates, not production authority roots.
forbidden: source observation upgraded into protection evidence

allowed_next_work: DMA/IOMMU/MemoryView implementation planning that names receipt sources
XSK/page-pool quarantine implementation planning that names packet generation reset
representor lower QueueLease derivation planning that names metadata, bridge, TC, and LAG proof events
service-work carrier planning that names per-effect carriers, merge semantics, and reset/rebuild reauthorization
VF mailbox carrier planning that names queue config, DMA ring base, IRQ route, queue budget, FDIR, async context, and revoke proofs
VF epoch handoff planning that names mailbox embargo, VF epoch bump, VSI generation, QueueLease generation, DMA/IRQ receipts, FDIR context clearing, and service replay authorization

HyperTag interface planning that names monitor-owned roots, service Domain policy-only authority, typed target endpoints, receipt minting, local cluster lease compilation, and no per-packet monitor trap
implementation planning that names subclaim coverage

root-management/local monitor observation-contract planning for LocalDomainDeviceLease rows, including cluster epoch, local monitor epoch, service Domain, target Domain, device root, DMA MemoryView, IRQ route, root budget, compile result, and revocation status
root-management/local monitor admission-protocol planning that consumes the LocalDomainDeviceLease observation contract without claiming enforcement
forbidden_next_work: Linux ring/q_vector/netdev/devlink state as non-forgeable authority
IOMMU/QueueLease protection claims without monitor-backed ownership
representor netdev, bridge FDB/VLAN, TC redirect target, or metadata_dst as lower QueueLease authority
worker identity, ICE_SERVICE_SCHED, virtchnl allowlists, PTP/DPLL callback reachability, LAG lower_dev rewrite, or reset/rebuild replay as service-work carrier authority
virtchnl validation, opcode allowlist, VF-provided dma_ring_addr, queue id check, vector id check, QoS caps, or FDIR ctx_done as VF mailbox carrier authority
vf_id equality, ice_vf pointer reachability, ICE_VF_STATE_ACTIVE/DIS, lan_vsi_idx/ctrl_vsi_idx stability, or VPINT/VPLAN programming success as VF epoch handoff authority
Linux DMA/IRQ state as monitor receipts
ClusterLease text, scheduler placement, service-domain admission, Linux PCI/devlink/IOMMU registration, or tracefs observation as LocalDomainDeviceLease authority
raw PF/VF/IOMMU/MSI/devlink authority exposed to target Domains
audit-only monitor calls after Linux side effects
per-packet monitor traps as the normal data path
